response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with strong web, testing, and some systems-level experience, but he misses nearly every core requirement of this role: there is no indication of Japanese proficiency (a native-level hard requirement), his C experience is brief and dated (a short stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015) with no Linux kernel investigation or modification work, and he shows no background in embedded networking, LAN switches, or security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or captive portals. His recent work is TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, which does not transfer to embedded firmware security development.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/robotics work
Systems-level exposure: low-latency asynchronous services in DLang, C/C++ components for LabWindows/CVI, Linux-based audio thesis project
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and teaching/workshop experience
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed; the role requires native-level Japanese
Weak match on core stack: minimal recent C for UNIX and no Linux kernel source-modification experience
No security or networking domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, SNMP/NETCONF) and no LAN-switch or firmware background

key_factors.factor: Broad senior engineering and systems experience
